name: Python
scope: An INTERMEDIATE Python developer, with 2-5 years experience, has a solid grasp of core programming concepts like data types, control flow, functions (including lambdas), and is adept with data structures such as lists, dictionaries, and sets. They are comfortable with object-oriented programming, able to create classes, implement inheritance, and understand polymorphism. They can manage modules and packages, use Python's standard library effectively, and handle third-party packages with tools like pip and virtual environments. Proficient in file operations and data serialization (JSON, CSV), they can interact with databases using SQL and ORMs like SQLAlchemy. They develop simple web applications using frameworks like Flask, consume and create APIs, and understand basic networking and concurrency (threading, multiprocessing). They write unit tests using unittest or pytest, debug code efficiently, and adhere to code quality standards like PEP 8 and best practices in documentation. Familiar with version control systems like Git, they collaborate using platforms like GitHub, participate in code reviews, and communicate effectively within a team. They can automate tasks with scripts, optimize code for performance, contribute to system design discussions, and improve existing systems while maintaining security awareness. Continuously learning, they stay updated with Python developments, seek feedback, and are capable of independent problem-solving on moderate-complexity projects.
long_scope:
An individual at the INTERMEDIATE proficiency level in Python is expected to have moved beyond the basics and can apply Python skills to solve real-world problems. They have a solid understanding of core concepts and can contribute meaningfully to projects. Below is a comprehensive scope of what an intermediate Python developer should be able to accomplish:

⸻

Core Programming Skills
	•	Data Types and Structures
	•	Proficient use of fundamental data types: integers, floats, strings, booleans.
	•	Advanced manipulation of data structures: lists, tuples, dictionaries, sets.
	•	Understanding of immutable vs mutable types.
	•	Control Flow
	•	Mastery of loops (for, while) and control statements (if, elif, else).
	•	Utilization of list comprehensions and generator expressions for efficient code.
	•	Functions
	•	Defining and invoking functions with positional, keyword, and default arguments.
	•	Understanding variable scope and lifetime.
	•	Use of anonymous functions (lambda).
	•	Modules and Packages
	•	Importing and organizing code using modules and packages.
	•	Familiarity with Python’s standard library modules (e.g., os, sys, datetime).
	•	Error and Exception Handling
	•	Using try, except, finally, and else blocks to handle exceptions.
	•	Raising exceptions and creating custom exception classes.

⸻

Object-Oriented Programming (OOP)
	•	Classes and Objects
	•	Creating classes with attributes and methods.
	•	Instantiating objects and accessing class members.
	•	Inheritance and Polymorphism
	•	Implementing single and multiple inheritance.
	•	Overriding methods and using super() to extend functionality.
	•	Understanding polymorphism and its application in Python.
	•	Encapsulation
	•	Using access modifiers to control access to class members.
	•	Understanding of getters and setters.

⸻

File and Data Handling
	•	File Operations
	•	Reading from and writing to text and binary files.
	•	Using context managers (with statement) for file operations.
	•	Data Serialization
	•	Working with JSON, CSV, and XML data formats.
	•	Serializing and deserializing data using json and pickle modules.
	•	Data Manipulation
	•	Using libraries like pandas and NumPy for data analysis.
	•	Performing data cleaning and transformation tasks.

⸻

Third-Party Libraries and Tools
	•	Package Management
	•	Installing and managing packages using pip.
	•	Understanding virtual environments (venv, virtualenv, conda) to manage dependencies.
	•	Popular Libraries
	•	Utilizing requests for HTTP operations.
	•	Implementing regular expressions with the re module.
	•	Basic plotting with matplotlib.

⸻

Web Development Basics
	•	Frameworks
	•	Building simple web applications using Flask.
	•	Understanding the basics of routing, templating, and handling HTTP requests.
	•	APIs
	•	Consuming RESTful APIs.
	•	Creating simple APIs to expose data or services.

⸻

Database Interaction
	•	SQL and NoSQL Databases
	•	Connecting to databases like SQLite or PostgreSQL.
	•	Performing CRUD operations using SQL queries.
	•	ORMs
	•	Using Object-Relational Mapping tools like SQLAlchemy to interact with databases.

⸻

Testing and Debugging
	•	Unit Testing
	•	Writing tests using unittest or pytest frameworks.
	•	Understanding test cases, test suites, and test runners.
	•	Debugging Techniques
	•	Using debugging tools or IDE features to step through code.
	•	Interpreting stack traces and logging output.

⸻

Version Control and Collaboration
	•	Git Proficiency
	•	Cloning repositories, committing changes, pushing, and pulling updates.
	•	Managing branches and understanding merge conflicts.
	•	Collaboration Tools
	•	Participating in code reviews using platforms like GitHub or GitLab.
	•	Documenting code changes and writing clear commit messages.

⸻

Code Quality and Best Practices
	•	PEP 8 Compliance
	•	Writing code that adheres to Python’s style guidelines for readability.
	•	Documentation
	•	Writing meaningful docstrings and inline comments.
	•	Generating documentation using tools like Sphinx.
	•	Refactoring
	•	Improving code structure and efficiency without altering external behavior.

⸻

Problem-Solving Skills
	•	Algorithmic Thinking
	•	Applying common algorithms and data structures to solve problems.
	•	Optimizing code for performance and scalability.
	•	Project Contributions
	•	Breaking down tasks into manageable components.
	•	Estimating time and resources required for development tasks.

⸻

Networking and Concurrency
	•	Basic Networking
	•	Understanding sockets and basic network communication.
	•	Working with higher-level network protocols.
	•	Concurrency
	•	Implementing threading and multiprocessing for concurrent execution.
	•	Understanding the Global Interpreter Lock (GIL) and its implications.

⸻

Automation and Scripting
	•	Task Automation
	•	Writing scripts to automate routine tasks (file management, data parsing).
	•	System Interaction
	•	Using Python to interact with the operating system and execute shell commands.

⸻

Security Awareness
	•	Secure Coding Practices
	•	Understanding common security vulnerabilities (e.g., injection attacks).
	•	Handling sensitive data securely (e.g., using environment variables).

⸻

Soft Skills and Teamwork
	•	Communication
	•	Effectively communicating technical concepts to team members.
	•	Continuous Learning
	•	Keeping up-to-date with the latest Python developments and libraries.
	•	Seeking feedback and actively improving coding skills.

⸻

Expected Tasks and Outcomes
	•	Develop Moderate-Complexity Applications
	•	Building applications that integrate multiple components (e.g., data processing pipeline).
	•	Maintain and Improve Existing Systems
	•	Debugging and fixing issues in existing codebases.
	•	Enhancing features and optimizing performance.
	•	Contribute to Design Discussions
	•	Participating in planning and architectural meetings.
	•	Providing input on technology choices and implementation strategies.

⸻

Tools and Technologies
	•	IDEs and Editors
	•	Proficient with tools like PyCharm, Visual Studio Code, or Sublime Text.
	•	Version Control Systems
	•	Comfortable using Git and platforms like GitHub.
	•	Databases
	•	Experience with relational databases (SQLite, PostgreSQL).
	•	Testing Frameworks
	•	Familiarity with unittest and pytest.
	•	Containers and Virtualization
	•	Basic understanding of Docker for containerization.

⸻

Mastery Over Concepts
	•	Efficient Coding
	•	Writing optimized and efficient code using appropriate data structures and algorithms.
	•	Modular Design
	•	Organizing code into reusable modules and packages.
	•	Error Handling
	•	Anticipating potential errors and writing robust code to handle exceptions gracefully.

⸻

Technical Expertise
	•	Independent Problem Solving
	•	Tackling assignments without constant supervision.
	•	Debugging Proficiency
	•	Quickly identifying and resolving code issues.
	•	Best Practices Application
	•	Implementing industry-standard practices in coding and development workflows.

⸻

Designing Systems and Processes
	•	System Design
	•	Contributing to the design of applications and systems.
	•	Process Improvement
	•	Suggesting and implementing improvements to development processes and workflows.

⸻

An intermediate Python developer is a valuable team member capable of handling a variety of programming tasks. They are not only able to write functional code but also contribute to the overall development process through effective communication, adherence to best practices, and a commitment to continuous learning.

name: Langchain
scope: A person with INTERMEDIATE proficiency level in Langchain as a competency should be able to / is capable of architecting end-to-end LLM applications by correctly combining LangChain Components, Chains, Agents and Applications, choosing between BaseLanguageModel, LLM and ChatModel classes and running them in sync, async or streaming modes. They design prompt workflows with PromptTemplate, ChatPromptTemplate and FewShotPromptTemplate, orchestrate dataflow with LangChain Expression Language primitives (run, pipe, map, flatten, invoke) and manage state through ConversationBufferMemory, TokenBufferMemory, VectorStoreRetrieverMemory or CombinedMemory. They ingest heterogeneous data via loaders such as Unstructured, BeautifulSoup4, pdfplumber, Notion, Jira, split text, embed with OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, VertexAI or HF models, index in FAISS, Chroma, Pinecone, Weaviate or Milvus and query through BaseRetriever, VectorStoreRetriever, MMR or Self-Query techniques while tuning chunk size, overlap and k. They assemble Sequential, SimpleSequential, Transform, Map-Reduce, Refine, Route-to-Expert, Split-Merge or Retrieval-Augmented-Generation chains, and implement ReAct, ZeroShot-ReAct-Description, StructuredChatZeroShot or OpenAI-functions agents equipped with PythonREPL, Calculator, SerpAPI, Tavily, SQLDatabase or custom REST/GraphQL tools. They handle callbacks and tracing with StdOutCallbackHandler, LangSmith or OpenTelemetry, implement retries, fallback models, exponential back-off, cache layers with InMemory, RedisCache or GPTCache, and enforce moderation, PII redaction, encryption-at-rest and policy guards. They write unit and integration tests using pytest, FakeLLM, StringEvaluator and CI pipelines on GitHub Actions or similar, capture quality with built-in QA, CoT or Criteria evaluators, and run automated regression gates. They expose apps via FastAPI, Streamlit or Gradio, package with Docker and Poetry/pip-tools, deploy to AWS Lambda, Azure Functions, K8s Helm charts or Terraform-provisioned vector stores, and optionally orchestrate jobs with Prefect, Airflow, Modal or Ray Serve. They profile latency, cost and token usage, balance model selection, retrieval parameters and memory footprint, and create dashboards or logs for stakeholders. They document prompt registries, tool wrappers and runbooks, mentor juniors on prompt engineering, retrieval tuning and memory usage, though they do not yet invent novel agent architectures or set organisation-wide LLM governance.
long_scope:
INTERMEDIATE-LEVEL COMPETENCY SCOPE  
(LangChain – approx. 3-5 years hands-on experience)

────────────────────────
1. Breadth & Depth of CONCEPTUAL UNDERSTANDING
────────────────────────
A. Core Abstractions & Data Flow  
• Components → Chains → Agents → Applications life-cycle; where, why, and how each fits.  
• LLMs vs. ChatModels vs. BaseLanguageModels; sync/async/streaming execution.  
• PromptTemplate, ChatPromptTemplate, Few-ShotPromptTemplate; dynamic variable injection.  
• LangChain Expression Language (LCEL) primitives (run, pipe, map, flatten, invoke).  
• Memory interfaces – ConversationBufferMemory, ConversationTokenMemory, VectorStoreRetrieverMemory, combined (CombinedMemory) patterns.  
• Retriever interface, BaseRetriever vs. VectorStoreRetriever; Filters, document metadata, hybrid retrieval, similarity vs. MMR.  
• Embeddings workflow – encoding, dimensionality, chunking, sliding windows, overlap trade-offs.  
• Vector Stores – architecture, persistence, indexing strategies, metadata schema.  
• Agents – ReAct, Zero-Shot-React-Description, StructuredChatZeroShot, OpenAI-functions agents; tool & toolkits contracts.  
• Tool interface – sync vs. async tools, JsonOutputParser, BaseTool vs. StructuredTool; building custom tools.  
• Callbacks/Event system – run managers, tags, nested runs, streaming tokens.  
• Evaluation – built-in evaluators (QA, CoT, Criteria-based), rubric design, human-in-the-loop.  
• Error handling & retry – RetryWithExponentialBackoff, fallback LLMs, guardrails integration.  

B. Architectural Patterns  
• Single-step vs. multi-step chains (SequentialChain, SimpleSequentialChain, TransformChain).  
• Map-Reduce, Refine, Route-to-Expert, Split-Merge summarisation patterns.  
• Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG): ingest pipeline → store → retrieve → generative synth.  
• Chat w/ Knowledge Base, Self-Query Retriever, Multi-vector retrievers.  
• Agent+Tool “act-observe” loops, terminating conditions, token budgeting.  

────────────────────────
2. TASKS THE INDIVIDUAL SHOULD ROUTINELY PERFORM
────────────────────────
A. Application Development  
✓ Prototype, iterate, and stabilise moderately complex conversational or document QA apps.  
✓ Stitch loaders → text splitters → embeddings → vector store via pipeline code or LCEL.  
✓ Construct custom Chains (e.g., summarise→retrieve→answer) that mix language, retrieval, and deterministic transforms.  
✓ Create domain-specific Agents with 3-10 tools (code-exec, search, calculator, SQL, custom REST).  
✓ Parameterise prompts for A/B experimentation; maintain prompt registries & versioning.  
✓ Build ingestion pipelines for PDFs, HTML, e-mails, Word, Notion, Jira.  
✓ Package as services (FastAPI) or lightweight demos (Streamlit/Gradio) with env-config.  

B. Quality Assurance & Evaluation  
✓ Write unit & integration tests using FakeLLM, StringEvaluator, or mock embeddings.  
✓ Apply automated regression evaluation in CI (accuracy, faithfulness, toxicity, latency).  
✓ Instrument with callbacks or LangSmith for tracing, latency heatmaps, token usage breakdown.  

C. Optimisation & Operations  
✓ Tune chunk size, k, MMR α; measure vs. F1 or BLEU; profile embeddings latency vs. cost.  
✓ Implement cache layers (In-Memory, RedisCache, GPTCache) and selectors for cold/hot paths.  
✓ Handle provider rate-limits, exponential back-off, parallel batch endpoints.  
✓ Implement API-key management, secret storage, and least-privilege IAM for hosted vector DBs.  

D. Security, Privacy, Governance  
✓ Redact PII before embedding; encrypt at rest; select private embeddings models when needed.  
✓ Enforce moderation & policy checks pre/post LLM with OpenAI/Guardian/Microsoft safety layers.  
✓ Comply with data residency requirements; understand SOC-2/HIPAA implications in tool choices.  

────────────────────────
3. EXPECTED OUTCOMES & DELIVERABLES
────────────────────────
• Stable, documented LangChain codebase following layering & dependency best-practices.  
• Reusable prompt templates and tool wrappers published as internal packages.  
• Deployment artefacts: Dockerfiles, Helm charts, Terraform for cloud vector stores.  
• Dashboards/trace logs demonstrating latency < X ms and answer quality > Y metric.  
• Readme and run-books enabling other engineers to reproduce, evaluate, and extend the pipeline.  

────────────────────────
4. TOOLING & TECHNOLOGIES THE INDIVIDUAL SHOULD COMMAND
────────────────────────
Language Runtimes  
• LangChain (Python mandatory, JS optional), asyncio, Pydantic v1/v2.  
LLM/Embedding Providers  
• OpenAI, Anthropic, Azure-OpenAI, HuggingFace, Cohere, Vertex AI.  
Vector Stores  
• FAISS (local), Chroma (local/cloud), Pinecone, Weaviate, Milvus; understand persistence vs. managed.  
Document Loaders & Converters  
• Unstructured, langchain.document_loaders.*, pdfplumber, BeautifulSoup4.  
Web & Execution Tools  
• SerpAPI, Tavily, DuckDuckGoSearchWrapper, PythonREPLTool, SQLDatabaseToolkit.  
Ops & Observability  
• LangSmith, OpenTelemetry, Sentry, Prometheus; tracing via StdOutCallbackHandler.  
Testing & CI  
• pytest, coverage, nox, GitHub Actions, LangChain testing helpers.  
Packaging & Deployment  
• Docker, Poetry/pip-tools, FastAPI, Streamlit/Gradio, Azure Functions, AWS Lambda.  
Orchestration (nice-to-have at intermediate)  
• Prefect, Airflow, Modal, Ray Serve.  

────────────────────────
5. SKILLS & BEHAVIORS DEMONSTRATING MASTERY AT THIS LEVEL
────────────────────────
• Chooses the correct chain vs. agent trade-off; articulates reasoning.  
• Diagnoses hallucinations by tracing prompt, context, and retrieval docs; iterates fix.  
• Balances token/latency/cost constraints when selecting models & retriever parameters.  
• Communicates limitations & risks of RAG solutions to stakeholders in clear terms.  
• Mentors juniors on prompt engineering, retries, and memory use without yet setting org-wide standards (reserved for advanced/expert).  

────────────────────────
6. WHAT IS NOT YET EXPECTED
────────────────────────
• Designing novel research-grade agent architectures.  
• Building cross-language bindings or contributing major PRs to LangChain core.  
• Owning enterprise-wide LLM governance strategy or multi-region infra scaleouts.  
• Deep optimisation of GPU-based embedding clusters or custom vector index algorithms.  

────────────────────────
This scope captures the complete set of concepts, tasks, tools, and expected outcomes an INTERMEDIATE LangChain practitioner should reliably handle, providing ample material for designing comprehensive interview questions.
